commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And he shall wash his flesh with water in the holy place, and put on his garments, and come forth, and offer his burnt offering, and the burnt offering of the people, and make an atonement for himself, and for the people. Flesh, which was, in some sort, defiled by touching the goat. Garments, belonging to his office. Come out of the holy of holies. (Calmet) The remainder of the day was spent in joy. The priest washed himself, as a sign that he had obtained pardon. (Menochius).
